title: active_role from template pack
purpose: B5.1 defines active role from template pack for the agent-driven expert system. Cognition & routing — [S0](B1.1-s0-deterministic-mandatory-first.md)–S4, conductor, workers, dual-stack.
scope: Owns `B5.1` only; siblings under `B5` must not duplicate this spec.
Aligns with minimal HITL: H1 plan, H2 blocker, H3 sign-off ([INTRO-1.2](INTRO-1.2-human-touchpoint-contract-h1-h2-h3.md)).
Conflicts resolve in favor of [Vision §4 — Branch B — Cognition & routing plane](../../full-automation-vision-and-hierarchy.md#4-branch-b-cognition-routing-plane).
behavior_steps: On pack instantiation ([F2.1](F2.1-company-instantiate-program-scoper-pack-select.md)), S0 loads `roles.yaml` from the active template-pack and sets `state.active_role` to the pack default for the current workstream—each role encodes pipeline_id, enabled skills, and tool permissions per [B5.2](B5.2-role-to-pipeline-id-skills-tool-permissions.md).

Each pursuit wake reads `active_role` before [A2.1](A2.1-preflight-check-pipeline-blocked-extended.md) preflight so route-tier, spawn policy, and HITL strictness match pack intent—not the conductor's improvised defaults.

When switching roles mid-goal, the conductor records manifest approval or an explicit journal decision, updates `active_role` in the same dual-write turn, and re-runs preflight so workers receive refreshed spawn contracts with role-scoped `allowed_reads`.

Under [A3.3](A3.3-company-autopilot-multi-goal-role-workstreams.md) company autopilot, scheduling rotates across roles via [F2.3](F2.3-company-active-role-rotates-ready-work.md) without merging permissions—each spawned worker inherits only its role's pipeline_id and tool allowlist.

If `active_role` is missing, references an undefined pack role, or conflicts with manifest lane bindings, pursuit stops at H2 until operators repair pack config or record a valid role switch—never routing implement phases with ambiguous role context.

narrative: B5.1 defines active role from template pack for the agent-driven expert system. Cognition & routing — [S0](B1.1-s0-deterministic-mandatory-first.md)–S4, conductor, workers, dual-stack. In practice, the system on pack instantiation ([f2.1](f2.1-company-instantiate-program-scoper-pack-select.md)), s0 loads `roles.yaml` from the active template-pack and sets `state.active_role` to the pack default for the current workstream—each role encodes pipeline_id, enabled skills, and tool permissions per [b5.2](b5.2-role-to-pipeline-id-skills-tool-permissions.md), then each pursuit wake reads `active_role` before [a2.1](a2.1-preflight-check-pipeline-blocked-extended.md) preflight so route-tier, spawn policy, and hitl strictness match pack intent—not the conductor's improvised defaults.
reader_narrative:
<!-- prose-source: agent plane-b 2026-06-28 -->

Active role comes from the instantiated template-pack: developer, operator, platform, legal reviewer—each with default pipeline_id and tool permissions. `state.json` carries active_role so pursuit, spawn policy, and HITL strictness match pack intent.

Switching roles mid-goal requires manifest or conductor decision recorded in journal; workers inherit role context in spawn contracts. Company autopilot ([A3.3](A3.3-company-autopilot-multi-goal-role-workstreams.md)) schedules across roles without merging their permissions.
